messages.content:
You are an expert Hindi linguist generating natural, varied example sentences for idiom senses.

Task:
For each sense, generate example sentences in Hindi with English translations.

Rules:
- Generate 3 sentences for idiomatic senses, 4 sentences for literal senses
- Each sentence must embed the idiom naturally with realistic surrounding context
- Context should feel like real speech or writing — include names, situations, backstory where natural.
  For example, rather than just "He let the cat out of the bag", write something like:
  "I told Mark not to say anything, but of course he let the cat out of the bag before I could surprise her."
- Vary morphology across sentences. Explicitly use different forms such as:
  - Different tenses (past, present, future, conditional)
  - Different persons (1st, 2nd, 3rd person)
  - Passive and active voice
  - Negation
  - Question forms
- Sentences should feel natural, not mechanical or repetitive
- Output ONLY valid JSON — no explanations, no markdown
- Format:
[
  {
    "idiom_id": "...",
    "sense_number": 1,
    "examples": [
      {"sentence": "...", "translation": "..."},
      ...
    ]
  }
]

DATA:

hi_unspecified_0092 | sense 2 | idiom: दम देना | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to support or remain with someone continuously; to be present for someone at every moment.

hi_unspecified_0092 | sense 3 | idiom: दम देना | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Culinary: to cook by the dum method; to slow-cook or steam food in a sealed pot so it cooks in its own steam.

hi_unspecified_0092 | sense 4 | idiom: दम देना | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative (violent): to cause death by suffocation or to finish someone off (used colloquially).

hi_unspecified_0093 | sense 1 | idiom: दम नाक में आना | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: for the breath to be affected in the nose; to feel one’s breathing obstructed.

hi_unspecified_0093 | sense 2 | idiom: दम नाक में आना | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to feel extreme irritation, exasperation, suffocation, or to be driven to the limits of patience or endurance.

hi_unspecified_0094 | sense 1 | idiom: दाल में कुछ काला होना | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literal: for there to be something black in the lentils; for the food to contain an unexpected dark spot or impurity.

hi_unspecified_0094 | sense 2 | idiom: दाल में कुछ काला होना | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Figurative: to suspect that something is amiss; to believe that there is something dishonest, hidden, or suspicious in a situation.

hi_unspecified_0095 | sense 1 | idiom: दिन में तारे देखना | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: To see stars during the daytime; a literal, physically unlikely observation.

hi_unspecified_0095 | sense 2 | idiom: दिन में तारे देखना | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: To entertain unrealistic hopes or fantasies; to be deluded or out of touch with reality.

hi_unspecified_0096 | sense 1 | idiom: दीये तले अँधेरा | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Darkness beneath a lamp; the literal contrast of a light source failing to illuminate its immediate surroundings.

hi_unspecified_0096 | sense 2 | idiom: दीये तले अँधेरा | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: A proverb meaning that people often fail to notice problems or obvious facts that are very close to them.

hi_unspecified_0097 | sense 1 | idiom: दूध का जला छाछ भी फूँक फूँक कर पीता है | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: One who has been scalded by hot milk blows even on buttermilk before drinking; a literal description of cautious behaviour after being burned.

hi_unspecified_0097 | sense 2 | idiom: दूध का जला छाछ भी फूँक फूँक कर पीता है | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Someone who, after suffering a bad experience, becomes excessively cautious or fearful in similar situations.

hi_unspecified_0098 | sense 1 | idiom: दूध का दूध और पानी का पानी | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literally, to separate milk from water; to distinguish pure milk from water when mixed.

hi_unspecified_0098 | sense 2 | idiom: दूध का दूध और पानी का पानी | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: To identify and make clear what is true and what is false, or to render a decisive, just resolution that separates right from wrong.

hi_unspecified_0099 | sense 1 | idiom: दूर के ढोल सुहावने | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Distant drums sound pleasant; a literal observation that sounds from afar may seem attractive.

hi_unspecified_0099 | sense 2 | idiom: दूर के ढोल सुहावने | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Things that are distant or unfamiliar often seem better or more appealing than those that are close or familiar; similar to 'the grass is greener on the other side.'

hi_unspecified_0100 | sense 1 | idiom: दूर के ढोल सुहावने लगते हैं | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: Drums heard from a distance sound pleasant and attractive.

hi_unspecified_0100 | sense 2 | idiom: दूर के ढोल सुहावने लगते हैं | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Things that are distant or unattainable often seem better than they actually are; the grass is greener on the other side.

hi_unspecified_0101 | sense 1 | idiom: दो नावों की सवारी | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: The act of riding on two boats at the same time.

hi_unspecified_0101 | sense 2 | idiom: दो नावों की सवारी | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Attempting to maintain two incompatible positions, duties, relationships, or loyalties simultaneously, often leading to failure or criticism.

hi_unspecified_0102 | sense 1 | idiom: न रहेगा बाँस न बजेगी बाँसुरी | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: If the bamboo is gone, the flute made from it cannot play.

hi_unspecified_0102 | sense 2 | idiom: न रहेगा बाँस न बजेगी बाँसुरी | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: When the source, means, or essential person is removed, the associated effects, functions, or benefits will also cease.

hi_unspecified_0103 | sense 1 | idiom: नक्राश्रु | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Without tears; tearless.

hi_unspecified_0103 | sense 2 | idiom: नक्राश्रु | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Emotionlessly stoic or uncomplaining; showing little or no outward grief or sentiment.

hi_unspecified_0104 | sense 1 | idiom: नया नौ दिन पुराना सौ दिन | type: literal_tentative | sentences: 4
Definition: A new thing lasts for nine days, while an old thing lasts for a hundred days.

hi_unspecified_0104 | sense 2 | idiom: नया नौ दिन पुराना सौ दिन | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Novelty is short-lived and fleeting, whereas established or familiar things endure longer.

hi_unspecified_0105 | sense 1 | idiom: नवरत्न | type: literal | sentences: 4
Definition: Literally, 'nine gems' or a collection of nine valuable jewels.

hi_unspecified_0105 | sense 2 | idiom: नवरत्न | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: A formal designation granted by the Government of India to certain Central Public Sector Enterprises that confers enhanced financial and operational autonomy.

hi_unspecified_0105 | sense 3 | idiom: नवरत्न | type: idiomatic | sentences: 3
Definition: Used figuratively to describe an organization, person, or thing regarded as especially prestigious, valuable, or exemplary.
